name: Financial Builders
description_short: Financial Builders is a not-for-profit credit union in Kokomo, IN offering checking, savings, lending, and credit card services as a member-owned cooperative.
description_long:
Financial Builders is a not-for-profit credit union headquartered in Kokomo, Indiana, operating as a member-owned financial cooperative rather than a traditional bank. The organization explicitly positions itself with the philosophy that "people should come before profits," emphasizing member benefits over shareholder returns typical of for-profit banks.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial services including checking and savings accounts (Kasasa Cash checking earning 5.25% APY up to $15,000), money market accounts, certificates of deposit with flexible withdrawal options, and retirement accounts (IRAs and HSAs). On the lending side, Financial Builders provides home loans including mortgages, FHA/VA loans, construction loans, and home equity products; consumer loans spanning auto, motorcycle, boat, RV, and powersport financing; personal loans including unsecured and consolidation options; and student loans. They also issue credit cards and provide comprehensive online banking with mobile deposit, bill pay, and e-statements.

What distinguishes Financial Builders is their emphasis on member ownership and competitive deposit rates. Their Kasasa Cash checking account offers notably high dividend rates (5.25% APY on balances up to $15,000), and their FLEX CD products allow penalty-free withdrawals under certain conditions—features designed to provide member flexibility. The organization actively promotes financial education through their advice library covering wealth management, saving and budgeting, credit management, and planning for the future.

As a credit union, Financial Builders operates on a cooperative principle where members are co-owners rather than customers of a for-profit entity. While their website demonstrates a full-service financial institution with competitive rates and member-centric products, potential members should note that credit union membership eligibility requirements apply, and service availability may be limited compared to national banks with thousands of branches. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Kasasa Cash checking accounts with dividend rates up to 5.25% APY
Flexible certificates of deposit with penalty-free withdrawal options
Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs) and Health Savings Accounts (HSAs)
Home mortgages, home equity loans, HELOCs, FHA/VA loans, and construction loans
Auto, motorcycle, boat, RV, and powersport vehicle financing
Personal unsecured loans and debt consolidation loans
Credit cards with Kasasa Cash Back rewards
Online banking with mobile deposit, bill pay, and e-statements
Credit card services including fraud dispute resolution and lost/stolen card replacement
pros: Kasasa Cash checking account earning 5.25% APY up to $15,000—significantly higher than typical checking account rates
FLEX CD products allow 1-2 penalty-free withdrawals during the term while maintaining the stated APY (3.75%-4.00%)
Member-owned not-for-profit structure means profits are returned to members rather than external shareholders
Comprehensive lending menu including specialized products like construction loans, lot loans, and powersport financing
Kasasa Cash Back rewards program that pays members for debit card purchases
Free online banking platform with mobile deposit, bill pay, and e-statements
Financial education resources through advice library covering budgeting, credit management, and wealth planning
cons: Credit union membership eligibility requirements apply—not all consumers automatically qualify to join
Physical presence limited to Kokomo, IN area, which may restrict in-person service access for distant members
Website content does not disclose loan approval rates, APR ranges, or lending qualification criteria for comparison
High-yield checking account requires specific qualification criteria and activity minimums (not disclosed on homepage)
Limited branch network compared to national banks, potentially fewer ATM locations through shared branching
best_for: Members in or near Kokomo, Indiana seeking high-yield checking and savings accounts with cooperative ownership benefits
Borrowers needing specialized lending products like construction loans, lot loans, or powersport financing
Consumers prioritizing member-owned financial institutions over for-profit banks and willing to meet membership requirements
Savers wanting flexible CD products with penalty-free withdrawal options and competitive dividend rates
diagnosis: Financial Builders is best for members in or near Kokomo, Indiana who qualify for credit union membership and seek competitive deposit rates, member-owned cooperative benefits, and comprehensive lending products. The primary caveat is that credit union membership eligibility requirements apply, and the organization's physical presence is limited to the Kokomo area, making it less accessible for geographically distant consumers.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
